text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet town nestled between rolling hills and ancient forests, there was a boy named Oliver. Oliver loved animals. He spent hours watching chickens peck at seeds, kittens explore boxes, and squirrels scamper up branches.

One day, Oliver decided he wanted a pet owl. He saved money collecting cans from garbage bins. With his dime-store owl puppet at his side, Oliver headed to the local wildlife reserve.

At the reserve, Oliver fed the birds. His favorite was an owl named Luna. One evening, Oliver built a cozy nest in a hollow tree and waited. Hours passed. Suddenly, Luna swooped down! Oliver shouted happily.

The owl swooped close. Oliver talked to Luna. They hooted together. That night, Oliver slept snugly in his nest while Luna hooted softly outside.

One sunlit morning, Oliver heard rustling. Something was there. Oliver peeked. A raccoon! He hadn’t fed Luna enough. Oliver grabbed
